title: Identification of fixed-time law with mu and descent of pathwise decomposition to mu-a.e. u

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** Prop. `decomp` and Steps 1-2.

The decomposition $u=l-C+v$ is a space-time (dynamic) object; the proof uses it as a statement about $\mu$-a.e. $u\in\mathcal D'$. Two facts are used without citation/proof: (i) the fixed-time marginal of the stationary dynamics is exactly the $\Phi^4_3$ measure $\mu$ (so 'a.s.' under the dynamics at fixed $t$ = '$\mu$-a.e.'), and (ii) the pathwise paracontrolled structure descends to a $\mu$-a.e. property of $u$. These should be cited precisely (HM18 / CC18 / EW24). Without (i)-(ii), Steps 1-2 do not directly bear on $\mu$ and $T_\psi^*\mu$.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved: identification of the fixed-time law with $\mu$ and descent of the pathwise decomposition to a $\mu$-a.e. statement.**

I rewrote the section "The decomposition of $\mu$" so that Prop. `decomp` is now stated as a **static** fact about a $\mu$-typical field, and is **proved** in three explicit steps that address exactly the two gaps flagged:

**Step (1)** ‚Äî Stationary dynamics. We invoke the global well-posedness of the renormalized $\Phi^4_3$ stochastic quantization equation $(\partial_t+1-\Delta)u=-\lambda\,{:}u^3{:}+\xi$ and the existence of a jointly stationary space‚Äìtime solution $u=\mathbf l-\mathbf C+v$ with paracontrolled remainder (HM18/CC18/EW24). This is the space‚Äìtime (dynamic) object the critic correctly distinguished from the static one.

**Step (2) ‚Äî point (i): fixed-time marginal $=\mu$.** Spelled out via ($\alpha$) $\mu$ is invariant for the dynamics (it is the weak limit of the Galerkin $\Phi^4$ Gibbs measures $\mu_N$, which are precisely the invariant measures of the truncated SDEs ‚Äî Glimm‚ÄìJaffe / CC18 / EW24), and ($\beta$) a stationary solution has the same law at every $t$, equal to an invariant measure; by uniqueness of the invariant measure (or simply by constructing the stationary solution started from $\mu$, as in EW24) this common law is $\mu$. Conclusion: $\mathbb P(u(0,\cdot)\in A)=\mu(A)$ for all Borel $A$ (Eq. `identif`). This is exactly the "a.s.-at-fixed-time = $\mu$-a.e." identification.

**Step (3) ‚Äî point (ii): descent to $\mu$-a.e. $u$.** All five components $\mathbf l(t,\cdot),\mathbf C(t,\cdot),v(t,\cdot),v^\sharp(t,\cdot),\mathbf q(t,\cdot)$ are a.s. time-continuous in their H√∂lder‚ÄìBesov spaces (HKN24/EW24), so evaluation at $t=0$ is a legitimate measurable operation and the paracontrolled identity holds at $t=0$ on the full-$\mathbb P$ event. I then form the augmented variable $\Theta=(u(0,\cdot);\mathbf l,\mathbf C,v,v^\sharp,\mathbf q)(0,\cdot)$, define the Borel set $\mathcal G\subset\mathcal D'\times E$ of tuples satisfying the algebraic relations **plus** the renormalized-product convergence $(\star)$ of Lemma `std` (Borel by the same argument as Lemma `borel`), note $\mathbb P(\Theta\in\mathcal G)=1$, and **disintegrate** $\nu:=\Theta_*\mathbb P$ over its first marginal $\mu$. Since $\nu(\mathcal G)=1$, the conditional law gives $\nu(\mathcal G\mid u)=1$ for $\mu$-a.e. $u$; hence for $\mu$-a.e. $u$ there exists a static decomposition with all properties used downstream. This produces the full-measure Borel set $G$ with $\mu(G)=1$.

I also added **Remark `descent`** making explicit that Steps 1‚Äì2 use **only** the static, fixed-time data ‚Äî no space‚Äìtime information beyond what Step (3) extracts ‚Äî so the proof now genuinely bears on $\mu$ and $T_\psi^*\mu$.

Minor: added a `\newtheorem{remark}` definition (the `remark` environment was used but undefined in the preamble). All environment/label/brace counts balance; no LaTeX toolchain available to compile.

Closing as resolved.
